title: ci: add Zizmor workflow
body:
Zizmor (https://docs.zizmor.sh/) is a static analysis tool for GitHub Actions. Most of the issues identified by Zizmor were fixed in #27642. This Zizmor action integrates with GitHub Advanced Security and scans workflows for potential security issues, which should help ensure that such issues aren't reintroduced in the future.

See also #27658, which is for the one remaining issue flagged by Zizmor (with default settings) that hasn't been fixed yet (and may or may not actually need remediation—if it doesn't, the relevant lines can be annotated so Zizmor ignores it).

#### Checklist

- [x] Certify you wrote the patch or otherwise have the right to pass it on as an open-source patch by signing all
commits. (`git commit -s`). (If needed, use `git commit -s --amend`).  The author email must match
the sign-off email address. See [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#sign-your-prs)
for more information.
- [x] Referenced issues using `Fixes: #00000` in commit message (if applicable)
- [x] [Tests](https://github.com/containers/podman/tree/main/test#readme) have been added/updated (or no tests are needed)
- [x] [Documentation](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/README.md) has been updated (or no documentation changes are needed)
- [x] All commits pass `make validatepr` (format/lint checks)
- [x] [Release note](https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/guide/release-notes.md) entered in the section below (or `None` if no user-facing changes)

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: This pull request sets up GitHub code scanning for this repository. Once the scans have completed and the checks have passed, the analysis results for this pull request branch will appear on [this overview](/containers/podman/security/code-scanning?query=pr%3A27663+is%3Aopen). Once you merge this pull request, the 'Security' tab will show more code scanning analysis results (for example, for the default branch). Depending on your configuration and choice of analysis tool, future pull requests will be annotated with code scanning analysis results. For more information about GitHub code scanning, check out [the documentation](https://docs.github.com/code-security/code-scanning/introduction-to-code-scanning/about-code-scanning).

Bumped actions/checkout to v6.0.1 in the Zizmor workflow. And no, #27658 doesn't need to be resolved before this merges—it just means that the use of those workflow triggers will be flagged in the "code scanning" section of the security tab, and I think will also be flagged in PRs that modify those workflows (but not in other PRs). So, essentially it'll serve as a reminder of the issue but won't block anything in the meantime.

> Bumped actions/checkout to v6.0.1 in the Zizmor workflow. And no, #27658 doesn't need to be resolved before this merges—it just means that the use of those workflow triggers will be flagged in the "code scanning" section of the security tab, and I think will also be flagged in PRs that modify those workflows (but not in other PRs). So, essentially it'll serve as a reminder of the issue but won't block anything in the meantime.

Cool, if that's the case, LGTM
/lgtm
Cockpit tests failed for commit f1f96facbe79769c62863d98d432fa3bb245c054. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.

> See also #27658, which is for the one remaining issue flagged by Zizmor (with default settings) that hasn't been fixed yet (and may or may not actually need remediation—if it doesn't, the relevant lines can be annotated so Zizmor ignores it).

@HastD Thanks for the PR. Just so I understand better, how is this being skipped in this PR to get the job to pass?

> it just means that the use of those workflow triggers will be flagged in the "code scanning" section of the security tab,

@HastD Is this expected to happen on subsequent PRs or on this PR as well?
Cockpit tests failed for commit 1dbb897733f3ead65edd0b939dd6ba75fb36da25. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.
[NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.

LGTM. 

I have one non-blocking concern that might come up. GitHub Actions has a limit on CI time for free-tier organizations (I think it is 2000 mins). That should be sufficient since we don't run many actions now, but if we increase our usage, we will need to address it.

@Honny1 Zizmor is pretty fast, so I don't think it would contribute significantly to that. Also, aren't public repositories exempt from that limit? I contribute to projects that use tens of thousands of minutes per month on GitHub Actions while still being on a free-tier organization.
Can Zizmor be run locally? Would be nice if we could add it to `make validatepr` so folks can find issues locally. If not, this is nonblocking, just a nice to have.
LGTM on the whole, more validation on our CI infra is useful.
/approve

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *ashley-cui*, *HastD* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [ashley-cui] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@mheon Yes, Zizmor can be run locally: https://docs.zizmor.sh/installation/

To integrate it with `make validatepr`, we'd need to figure out what installation method to use—maybe via their container image? Certainly doable though.
Putting it in the `validatepr` container image would probably be best. @baude Where does the Containerfile for that live?
Here: https://github.com/baude/podman/blob/1bddd38e0ac6017e7a680c6f5e7532659be8549d/contrib/validatepr/Containerfile

> @Honny1 Zizmor is pretty fast, so I don't think it would contribute significantly to that. Also, aren't public repositories exempt from that limit? I contribute to projects that use tens of thousands of minutes per month on GitHub Actions while still being on a free-tier organization.

Correct, in the past I had the same concern because in the repo settings it looked like 2000/2000 minutes of our quota had been used on another project but yeah _"GitHub Actions usage is free for self-hosted runners and for public repositories that use standard GitHub-hosted runners."_

https://docs.github.com/en/billing/concepts/product-billing/github-actions#how-use-of-github-actions-is-measured
